title: High-Molecular-Weight SPRI-aided DNA extraction from Mimulus (Phrymaceae) leaf tissue
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"25jug","text":"This protocol is adapted from Russo et al. 2023 methods for high-molecular weight DNA extraction. I modified the protocol to use reagents and incubation conditions used by the Ferris Lab at Tulane University to extract DNA from Mimulus spp. (monkeyflower) tissue (leaf and buds), see protocol. The original CTAB:Chloroform protocol used by the Ferris lab traces back to the Willis Lab at Duke University. The objective of these DNA extractions are to collect fungal DNA present in leaf tissue. I have incorporated sample preparation procedures used by the Arnold Lab at The University of Arizona to eliminate contamination of samples and optimize DNA extractions from preserved leaf tissue for future fungal ITS Illumina MiSeq sequencing. Solid phase reversible immobilization beads (SPRI) utilized are adapted from Rohland and Reich 2012 and Liu et al 2023. All extractions were performed in a sterile biosafety cabinet to prevent sample contamination.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":228,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":30,"length":17},{"key":1,"offset":501,"length":65},{"key":2,"offset":816,"length":22},{"key":3,"offset":843,"length":14}],"data":{}},{"key":"1iami","text":"\nPrior to being placed in CTAB, photosynthetic tissue from plants was surface sterilized with sequential washes in 95% EtOH (10 s) , 0.5% sodium hypochlorite (1 min), and 70% EtOH (1min) and air dried under sterile conditions. Due to the small size of monkeyflower plants, the maximum amount of leaf tissue produced per host were placed in 750- 1000 µL of CTAB buffer and kept at RT until DNA extraction process.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bcomg","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6pjsh","text":"*Several aspects of this protocol, mainly those with reagent amounts and molarities, have changed compared to the Russo et al 2023 protocol and the Willis Lab standard CTAB extraction protocol from ca. 2010.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eep4n","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"amfqo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"024EA393177811EEA9550A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fpls.2022.883897/full"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"024EA3D5177811EEA9550A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/96-well-dna-extraction-protocol-for-plant-and-lich-fscbnaw"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"72C67E20185211EE89CD35C6FD1390E7","url":"https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22267522/"}},"3":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"6622DDD0185211EE89CD35C6FD1390E7","url":"https://doi.org/10.1186/s12864-023-09211-w"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3c85a","text":"General Notes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fvkq1","text":"DNA Extractions for Illumina Seq. are the most sensitive. If you need high quality low contamination DNA that is high molecular weight, make sure to use the gentler methods of mixing. Use wide bore pipet tips to help reduce shearing of DNA. Do not vortex any samples to mix, just invert or gently tap/flick tubes. To reduce contamination, be sure to check that all samples come out of the first chloroform step with a clear aqueous layer, not cloudy. Sodium chloride (5M NaCl) helps reduce polysaccharide contamination (compared to the Isopropanol precipitation without the added NaCl). High salt concentrations also aid in DNA precipitation, so you should get more DNA precipited and cleaned with chloroform wash. ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3uasp","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f7873","text":"Sample Handling ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bk5bg","text":"Gloves must be worn at all times. Change gloves frequently and decontaminate often with DNA Away.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"align-justify","offset":0,"length":97}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cph94","text":"Pipetting must be done extremely carefully to minimize the risk of aerosols that can easily cause contamination between samples.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c5abl","text":"Use only unopened, sterile, aerosol-resistant pipette tips (filter tips) to minimize contamination of the pipette shaft and your samples. Clean the body of the pipettes regularly with DNA Away.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7g4iu","text":"Discard pipette tips after each use to avoid cross-contamination. Eject pipette tips carefully to prevent aerosol formation or other splashing.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4i9hf","text":"Remember to always clean the hood and any equipment with DNA Away and decontaminate where possible with UV for 30 minutes prior to use to avoid cross-contamination.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9s22t","text":"The pipettes used need to be calibrated annually.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2765t","text":"Be careful not to touch the inside lid of tubes as it will cross-contaminate samples.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d0ai1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"976ij","text":"Careful handling all reagents, specially CTAB and Chlorofom:IAA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Clean the biosafety cabinet, pipettes, tip boxes and any other instrument to be used with 70% EtOH, 10 % Bleach and 95 % EtOH. Surface sterilize all instruments in biosafety cabinet with UV light for 00:30:00 .
Quickly after placing tissue in tubes, place tube cap and close tightly. Don’t let samples sit at room temperature for very long because the DNA will degrade.
Place tubes in the -20 °C or -80 °C freezer. Until ready for freeze drying/lyophilization step.
Once all your samples are cold, turn on the refrigerator for the lyophilizer and place the glass plate on the front. Make sure there is no water in the chamber or drain tube.
Weigh empty 2 mL tube to determine how much tissue you need to place in tube. Screw cap micro tubes 2 mL Thermo Scientific Catalog #3469NK

Transfer 0.15-0.3 grams of leaf tissue to new 2 mL tube. Leaving remaining leaf tissue in original 15 mL vial tube. This step depends on how easy to handle your plant tissue is. Fresh tissue is easier to handle. Mimulus spp. tissue preserved in CTAB turns "mushy" and it is difficult to handle and transfer. Screw cap micro tubes 2 mL Thermo Scientific Catalog #3469NK
Weigh 2 mL tube with leaf tissue to determine if it has the correct amount. Samples with more tissue (> 0.3 <0.6) can be split into two tubes.
Once the lyophilizer temperature is at -40 °C , turn on the vacuum pump. Vacuum pressure must be below 150 milliTorr . If pressure doesn't drop within the hour do not use machine.
When the pressure is at the appropriate level, release the pressure by turning the white port 180 degrees. Make sure there is a vacuum seal, and the pressure returns to .

Lyophilize samples for a minimum of 48:00:00 . To remove the samples, first turn the white port on the lyophilizer 180 degrees to release the pressure in the chamber. Once the pressure is released and the gauge reads 0, turn off the vacuum pump and then the refrigerator. Remove the glass plate from the front and wipe out the water after it has melted (also shake out the drain tube).
Quickly take frozen tubes out of the freezer and place inside the lyophilizer chamber. Close the lid and close the port.

In a sterile laminar flow hood, remove the parafilm or lid from the tubes (being careful not to touch the inside lip of the cap) and add beads for homogenization. For all plant samples except mosses, add the following: The pulverization/homogenization of tissue can be performed beforehand and samples stored away until DNA extraction. I prefer to pulverize all my samples, store them and work my way through the DNA extraction.
Tubes with beads can be placed in a -20 °C freezer until ready to finish pulverization steps.

Dip tubes/96-well plates in liquid nitrogen. a) Make sure there are no cracks in the plastic and that the plate has its bottom panel to keep the tubes and racks from shattering. b) Clamp plates into shaker rack in Geno Grinder 2010 or TissueLyser LT. Always clamp plates into both shaker racks and make sure tubes are evenly distributed across plates.

Shake at 1500 strokes/minute for 3 minutes in GenoGrinder 2010 or 25-30 Hz for 3 minutes if using TissueLyzer LT. Tissuelyser LT (Qiagen, 85600, Tissue and cell lyser, https://www.qiagen.com/us/products/discovery-and-translational-research/dna-rna-purification/instruments-equipment/tissuelyser-lt?catno=85600 ) a) Decrease or increase time as necessary. b) Check periodically and do not grind for longer than necessary. Do not let tissue thaw, if you notice melting, re-dip in liquid N2. c) When tissue is ground to a fine powder, dip briefly into liquid nitrogen again to keep frozen. d) Examine tubes carefully to ensure proper pulverization. Repeat if necessary.

Centrifuge plate fast and briefly at 4000 rpm, , when using 96-well plate centrifuge or 10.000 rpm, , when using Corning LSE centrifuge to get powder off lids. Anywhere from 1-3 minutes depending on how much the sample adheres to the lid of the tube.
Store in -20 °C or -80 °C until DNA extraction steps.

Add 100 μL (2 beads) of the autoclaved 3.2 mm stainless steel beads. Stainless stee; beads 3.2mm Next Advance Catalog #SSB32 Beads can also be added at the same time sample tissue is weighed and transffered to 2 mL tubes. Organize your workflow as you see fit.
Add 2-3 of the autoclaved 2 mm zirconium oxide beads to each tube with a sterile scoop. Stainless stell bead blend 0.9-2mm Next Advance Catalog #SSB14B
Add 100 μL of the autoclaved stainless steel bead blend, (0.9-2.0mm) using a sterile scoop. Close the lids securely. Ziconium oxide beads Next Advance Catalog #ZROB20-RNA

Surface sterilize all workbench surfaces and instruments with a 10% solution of 0.5% sodium hypochlorite, 70% EtOH and 95% EtOH prior to placement in biosafety cabinet. Surface sterilize allinstruments in biosafety cabinet with UV light for 00:30:00 .

Make Extraction Master Mix (Pre-warm to 60 ° C):Measure out 700 µL of CTAB extraction buffer per sample into 15-50 mL conical centrifuge tube. Add 2.25 µL β-mercaptoethanol per 700 µL of CTAB extraction buffer to the same conical tube (0.3% of the total extraction buffer volume). Basic recipe: [(# of samples x 1.1) x 700 µL of CTAB] + [(# of samples + 1) x 2.25 µL β-mercaptoethanol] See Guidelines and Warning for more information. 2-mercaptoethanol Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #M6250 CTAB buffer OPS Diagnostics Catalog #CEB 500-02 Polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP-40) VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #0507 (CAS: 9003-39-8)
After 20 minutes incubation in step 18, add 4 µL of RNase A . RNase A 10mg/ml, DNase and Protease-free Thermo Scientific Catalog #EN0531

Add 700 µL of preheated Extraction Master Mix to each tube, replace the lids (if using strip tubes). a) Shake tubes by hand vigorously until tissue is thawed and fully suspended in the buffer. Dip plate or tubes in hot water bath to assist the thaw if needed. b) If fine tissue powder is collected at the bottom and it does not mix well with CTAB buffer, then place in Vortex 5-30 seconds (depending on tissue) or TissueLyser LT for 1 minute at 15 Hz. Beware of DNA shearing. Always make sure to include an extraction blank (negative control).

Incubate tubes for 00:20:00 in a 60 °C water bath. Important: Invert tubes/plates gently about 10 times every ~10 minutes during incubation. If using strip tubes: Keep costar plate lid off during water bath and grinding step (prior) and refasten strip tube caps before inverting – they will pop off! Keep fingers firmly on strip tube lids while inverting.
Incubate at 60 °C for 00:10:00 . Invert 10 times once during incubation.

All 200 µL of 5M NaCl (or 5M Sodium/Potassium acetate). To fully precipitate proteins and polysaccharides in extraction buffer. Slowly pipette up and down or invert 25 times. Sodium Chloride Fisher Scientific Catalog #S271
Add 900 µL of chloroform/isoamyl (24:1) (1 volume) to each tube.

Place tubes in a nutating mixer for 00:10:00 at 24 rpm. a) If possible, use instruments that can produce 10-20 rpms. Less shaking = less shearing. b) Can invert by hand 20 - 40 times, instead of shaking, to furtherdecrease shear. Wear extra thick nitrile gloves to prevent chloroformexposure to skin. If you get chloroform on your gloves, remove them as soon aspossible and discard them in the solid hazardous waste bag in the hood! S0500 Mini Nutating Mixer (VWR, 82007-202, Mixer, https://us.vwr.com/store/product/4787436/null )

Centrifuge for 00:10:00 at 4000rpm when 96-well plate centrifuge or 00:10:00 at 10.000 rpm, , when using Corning LSE centrifuge. A band of tissue debris will separate the aqueous (upper) and chloroform/organic (lower) layers. The top aqueous layer should be clear, not cloudy. If cloudy, go back and re-do steps 23 and 24.

Carefully pipette off ~ 800 µL - 900 µL of the top aqueous layer using filter pipet tips andtransfer to sterile new tubes or 96-well plate. Avoid drawing up debris or chloroform from the middle and lower layers (if so, re-centrifuge briefly and transfer again). Eppendorf DNA low-bind microtube 2 mL Fisher Scientific Catalog #022431048
Repeat steps 22-24 go to step #22 . This will remove any leftover RNase and contaminants.

After repeating steps 22-24. Carefully pipette ~ 700 µL - 800 µL of the top aqueous layer using wide-bore filter pipet tips and transfer to sterile new tubes or 96-well plate. a) Avoid drawing up debris or chloroform from the middle and lower layers (if so, re-centrifuge briefly and transfer again). b) You can cut 3-4 mm of P1000 tips to make yourself "wide-bore" tips. Do this beforehand in a sterile environment or autoclave tips afterwards. Eppendorf DNA low-bind microtube 2 mL Fisher Scientific Catalog #022431048 Feel free to pause and get yourself ready for DNA purification. DNA is stable at this stage and can be left at room temperature while you prepare.
Turn ON heating block to 50 °C and place 10 mM Tris-HCl or 1X TE buffer to pre-heat. 1X TE buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0 1 mM EDTA) Contributed by users 10 mM Tris-HCL pH 8.0 Contributed by users

Add SPRI bead mixture according to the following: Volume of SPRI beads to add = 1.2 × DNA volume (from step 27) Adjust the ratio of SPRI beads as you see fit. Russo et al 2023 use 0.7x. See Rohland and Reich 2013 and Liu et a 2023 for SPRI protocol and A B C D 1 Final concentration for a 1.25% SPRI bead solution for use in DNA extraction process. 2 Reagent 10 mL 50 mL Final Concentration 3 Sera-Mag Speed Beads 125 µL 625 µL 1.25% 4 PEG-8000 2.5 g 12.5 g 25% 5 5 M NaCl 1000 µL 25 mL 2.5 M 6 1 M Tris-HCl (pH 8.00 100 µL 500 µL 10 mM 7 0.5 M EDTA (pH 8.00 20 µL 100 µL 1 mM 8 10% Polysorbate 20 (Tween 20) 5.5 mL 275 mL 10 % 9 Nuclease free H2O fill to 10 mL fill to 50 mL n/a SeraMag SpeedBeads Carboxyl-Magnet-Beads hydrophob Fisher Scientific Catalog #GE Healthcare 65152105050250 Polyethylene glycol (PEG-8000) Fisher Scientific Catalog #FLBP2331 EDTA Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #E9884 Polysorbate 20 (Tween-20) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P9416
Incubate for 00:30:00 in a nutating mixer at 24 rpms (default).
Spin down for 00:00:05 on centrifuge.

Place the tubes on a magnetic rack for 00:05:00 or wait until thesolution is clear. This can take several minutes depending on the viscosity and type of sample. Wait until the solution is clear before proceeding to the next step.

Aspirate the cleared solution from the tubes, inspect pipette tips for bead residue, and discard. This step must me performed while the tubes are placed on the magnetic rack. Avoid disturbing the settle magnetic beads. If beads are drawn into tips, leave behind a few microliters of solution..

Dispense 1 mL of fresh 80% ethanol to each tube and invert 25 times to resuspend bead pellet. Flick tubes gently to help separate the beads from the tube wall. Avoid prolonged incubation in EtOH. Fresh 80% Ethanol Contributed by users
Spin down for 1-2 s on centrifuge.
Place tubes on magnetic rack .
Aspirate out the ethanol, inspect pipette tips for bead residue, and discard.
Repeat for a total of three washes. go to step #33
Let beads air-dry for 00:01:00 after final wash.

Take tubes out of the magnetic rack, add 50 μL of elution buffer (10 mM TRIS-HCl pH 8.0,or 1X TE buffer) pre-heated to 50 °C to each tube and pipette mix 5 times. 1X TE buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0 1 mM EDTA) Contributed by users 10 mM Tris-HCL pH 8.0 Contributed by users
Incubate tubes at 37 °C for 00:15:00 in benchtop shaking incubator at 20 rpm. If possible, use instrument that can produce 10-20 rpms.
Spin down for 1-2 s on centrifuge.
Place tubes in magnetic rack and let sit for 00:15:00 - 00:30:00 . I've had best results with 30 minutes.
Collect ~ 50 µL of supernatant and transfer to a new tube.
Place in tube stand and let sit at RT overnight . This will allow the DNA to homogenize and relax. You can relax too!
Store DNA at -20 °C or -80 °C until ready for quantification with Qubit or PicoGreen assay and downdtreams analyses.
